topics: 📜 קוצים, גדיש, קמה, שדה — Torah's four words for fire targets
🌬️ Wall, road, river — when does the fire's spread break liability?
📏 16 / 50 / bet kor — how far must a fire spread to count as 'sent'?
🥬 Mishnah 6:7 — implements in a stack: R' Yehudah vs. Chachamim
🐐 The kafut goat and the nearby slave — capital crime or restitution?

overview.title: 📖 Overview — How Far Does Fire Travel?
overview.label: The sugya:
overview.text: The daf walks through the Torah's strange list of fire-damage objects (kotzim, gadish, kamah, sadeh) and shows how each word stops you from over-restricting the others. Then comes the geography: a wall four amos high, a road, or a river — does the fire-setter still pay if his fire crossed those? R' Abba and R' Yochanan split on whether the Mishnah is about a 'burning' fire or a 'wind-whipped' fire. Then Mishnah 6:6's distance debate (16, 50, or 'bet kor center'), capped by the famous Mishnah of the goat tied to the stack and the slave standing nearby — where capital crime exempts payment.
overview.hebrewSource: תַּלְמוּד לוֹמַר אוֹ הַשָּׂדֶה ... רוֹאִין אוֹתָהּ כְּאִילּוּ הִיא בְּאֶמְצַע בֵּית כּוֹר

slides.title: 📜 Why Four Words for Fire Targets?
slides.subtitle: Each Torah term blocks a different over-restriction.
rows: Only flammable; not the field, furrow, or stones

callout.label: Why all four:
callout.text: Without 'sadeh,' you'd exempt damage to bare furrow or stone. Without 'kamah,' you'd exempt growing crops. The Yerushalmi shows each word covers a gap — and asks how Chachamim, who limit liability to wheat/barley stacks, square that with the word 'sadeh.'

slides.title: 💬 The Wall, the Road, the River
slides.subtitle: When does a fire's leap break the chain?

lines.speaker: M
lines.text: עָבְרָה גָדֵר שֶׁהוּא גָבוֹהַּ אַרְבַּע אַמּוֹת אוֹ דֶרֶךְ הָרַבִּים אוֹ נָהָר פָּטוּר — crossed a 4-amos wall, road, or river → exempt.

lines.speaker: R' Abba
lines.text: The Mishnah is about קוֹדַחַת — a burning fire. But a wind-whipped fire (מְקַטֶּפֶת) — everyone agrees you're liable, because the wind extends your causation.

lines.speaker: R' Y
lines.text: Inverse: the Mishnah is about מְקַטֶּפֶת. A burning fire (קוֹדַחַת) — exempt, because steady fire stays put.

callout.label: Behind the dispute:
callout.text: Whether the human's fire-act extends through wind. R' Abba treats wind as your tool; R' Yochanan treats it as a break. Either way the wall/road/river is the geographical limit case.

slides.title: 📏 The Distance Debate — Mishnah 6:6
slides.subtitle: How far does fire have to travel to count?

boxes.title: 🌐 R' Eleazar b. Azaryah
items: Imagine the fire at the center of a bet kor
Liability based on whether it could reasonably reach there

boxes.title: ⚖️ R' Shimon
items: Case-by-case based on the fire's reality

callout.label: From the case file:
callout.text: מַעֲשֶׂה בָּעֲרָב שֶׁקָּפַץ הָאוֹר יוֹתֵר מִשְּׁלֹשׁ מֵאוֹת אַמָּה — in Arabia a fire jumped over 300 amos. The numbers in the Mishnah cap normal liability; extreme fires need R' Shimon's flexible rule. נָהָר אוֹ שְׁלוּלִית רְחָבִין שְׁמוֹנֶה אַמּוֹת ends liability.

slides.title: 🥬 Hidden Items in the Stack
slides.subtitle: The R' Yehudah / Chachamim debate runs in opposite directions.

columns.label: R' Yehudah
columns.content: יְשַׁלֵּם כָּל מַה שֶׁבְּתוֹכוֹ — pays for everything inside

columns.label: Chachamim
columns.content: אֵינוֹ מְשַׁלֵּם אֶלָּא גָדִישׁ חִטִּים אוֹ שְׂעוֹרִים — only the wheat/barley stack

nodes.content: BUT — Chachamim agree with R' Yehudah on threshing-floor implements (כלי גורן) since they're normally hidden there
And both sides agree: burning a building → pays for everything inside — since storage is normal in homes

callout.label: R' Hoshaya's harmonization:
callout.text: When there are no witnesses to what was hidden, the dispute remains. But with witnesses, all agree the victim collects per the proven contents — even by oath in cases of disputed sack contents.

slides.title: 🐐 The Kafut Goat and the Nearby Slave
slides.subtitle: Where capital liability swallows monetary liability.

steps.content: 1️⃣ Goat tied to the stack, slave nearby: both burn → fire-setter חַיָיב.
2️⃣ Slave tied to the stack, goat nearby: both burn → fire-setter פָּטוּר.
3️⃣ Why the difference? In case 2, killing the slave is a capital crime.
4️⃣ Principle: קַם לֵיהּ בְּדְרַבָּה מִינֵּיהּ — when one act triggers both capital and monetary liability, the capital absorbs the monetary.
5️⃣ Result: the fire-setter walks free of even the goat's value, because his single act is on the death track.

callout.label: Sharp consequence:
callout.text: The slave's life situation determines the goat's price tag. Halakhah doesn't subdivide one act of arson into separate liabilities — the heaviest consequence absorbs the rest.

summary.principle: Fire liability is built from layered Torah words (kotzim → sadeh) and bounded by physical breaks (wall, road, river). Within those, the fire-setter pays for what is normally there — including hidden items in homes — until capital liability for a tied human absorbs the entire act.

flowSteps.content:
🎓 Key Principle:
Tort liability for fire tracks the realistic reach of a person's act — and capital concerns swallow monetary ones when both apply.

quiz.q: Why does the Torah list קוצים, גדיש, קמה, AND שדה?
o: Each word blocks an over-narrow reading the others would allow
The four match the four avot
Each refers to a different season
quiz.e: אוֹ הַשָּׂדֶה in particular catches damage to the field's bare ground (furrow, stone) that flammable-only categories would miss.

quiz.q: According to R' Abba, when does the Mishnah's wall/road/river exempt the fire-setter?
o: Only for a burning fire (קוֹדַחַת); a wind-whipped fire (מְקַטֶּפֶת) — חייב
quiz.e: R' Abba treats wind-driven fire as the setter's extended causation, so the wall doesn't break liability.

quiz.q: What is R' Akiva's threshold for fire spread?
quiz.e: חֲמִשִּׁים אַמָּה ... בִּשְׁעַת הָרוּחַ מֵאָה אַמָּה.

quiz.q: On hidden items in a burned stack, what do Chachamim hold?
o: Pays only for wheat or barley sheaves — not hidden כלים
Pays only the value of the stack itself
quiz.e: אֵינוֹ מְשַׁלֵּם אֶלָּא גָדִישׁ חִטִּים אוֹ גָדִישׁ שְׂעוֹרִים. R' Yehudah disagrees and includes hidden items.

quiz.q: Why is the fire-setter exempt when the slave was tied to the stack and the goat was nearby?
o: The slave's life is more valuable
קַם לֵיהּ בְּדְרַבָּה מִינֵּיהּ — capital liability absorbs monetary liability
quiz.e: When the same act incurs both death penalty (for the slave) and monetary damage (for the goat), the heavier liability absorbs the lighter.
